name: go
description: Go stack: context propagation, goroutine safety, small interfaces, table-driven tests, error wrapping.

rules.guidance: Pass context.Context as the first argument to every function that performs I/O or calls across service boundaries. Never store context in a struct field.
Document goroutine safety on exported types. Shared mutable state must use sync primitives or channels. Prefer sharing memory by communicating.
Define interfaces at the point of use with the minimum methods needed (1-3 methods is idiomatic). Avoid large interfaces that embed unrelated behaviors.
Use table-driven tests ([]struct{...}) for functions with multiple input/output combinations. Each case must have a name field for readable test output.
Wrap errors with context using fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err). Return errors; do not log and return. Callers decide how to handle or log.
